political.s: BetterHelp (owned by Teladoc Health) has no significant political giving record. The company has lobbied on telehealth regulation.

charity.s: BetterHelp has offered reduced-cost or free services to some underserved populations. The company does not have a substantial formal charitable giving program.

environment.s: BetterHelp has minimal direct environmental footprint as a telehealth platform. No significant environmental commitments disclosed.

labor.s: BetterHelp has been widely criticized for paying therapists extremely low rates — as little as $30–$40 per hour including administrative time — well below standard private practice rates of $100–200/hour. Therapists report pressure to take on more clients than is clinically advisable.

dei.s: BetterHelp markets its ability to match patients with therapists of similar backgrounds, including race, religion, and LGBTQ+ identity. However, a 2022 investigation found some therapists on the platform expressed anti-LGBTQ+ views to patients before being removed.

guns.s: BetterHelp does not sell or manufacture firearms.

privacy.s: The FTC fined BetterHelp $7.8M in March 2023 for sharing users' sensitive mental health data with Facebook, Snapchat, Pinterest, and Criteo for advertising purposes without adequate disclosure — a serious violation of user trust given the sensitivity of mental health information.

execPay.s: BetterHelp is a subsidiary of Teladoc Health; standalone executive compensation not disclosed.
